repo_architecture_overview: This repository uses a layered architecture for request handling and agent execution. Architecture: API interfaces route requests through orchestration and specialized agents, with infrastructure modules handling model routing, retrieval, and persistence. Entry points: jarvis_main.py, main.py. Key modules: interfaces, agents, core, infrastructure, memory. Important flows: inbound API request -> model/route decision -> specialized capability execution -> composed response with safety checks. Top directories in sample: interfaces, agents, core, infrastructure. Dependency files: requirements.txt, pyproject.toml. Route-related files: interfaces/api_interface.py. Evidence: Core entrypoints and module boundaries are directly observable in startup and API wiring files. Sources: jarvis_main.py, main.py, interfaces/api_interface.py. Confidence: 0.85. Coverage: 42.0% of repository files. Risks: Incomplete integration coverage across all edge execution paths. Next steps: Add deeper end-to-end tests for routing and verifier behavior.
repo_runtime_flow: The runtime flow is API-driven with orchestration and capability dispatch. Architecture: requests are parsed at the API layer, transformed into typed task payloads, dispatched to orchestrator, then executed by specialist agents before final response composition. Entry points: jarvis_main.py, interfaces/api_interface.py. Key modules: interfaces, core, agents, infrastructure. Important flows: request parse -> route/policy decision -> capability handler -> response composer and telemetry. Evidence: API and orchestration handoff is visible in interface and agent modules. Sources: interfaces/api_interface.py, core/orchestrator.py, agents/specialized_agents.py. Confidence: 0.8. Coverage: 35.5% of repository files. Risks: Some claims still rely on sampled-file inference rather than exhaustive trace. Next steps: Expand depth run and verify additional call paths.
repo_risk_assessment: Primary hardening risks are evidence gaps, execution-path regressions, and environment drift between host/container runs. Architecture: system is modular but depends on consistent contracts between API parsing, orchestration, and specialist agents. Entry points: jarvis_main.py, main.py. Key modules: interfaces, agents, core, infrastructure. Important flows: command parsing -> workspace resolution -> task dispatch -> verification-aware response composition. Evidence: Risk assessment aligns with observable contract and routing files. Sources: interfaces/api_interface.py, agents/specialized_agents.py, core/config.py. Confidence: 0.78. Coverage: 33.0% of repository files. Risks: unsupported claims may appear if evidence policy is bypassed | path mapping mismatch can break repository tasks. Open questions: Should contradiction detection be made fail-closed for high-risk outputs? Next steps: enforce CI quality gates and expand contradiction regression tests.
